guardrailFacts.corePathDefinition: npm run check failed, or at least two core acceptance items failed at both viewports.

findings.title: Two destination save controls are enabled but inert
findings.evidence: Every destination renders an enabled `Save {destination.name}` button, but the click handler and saved styling only act when `index === 0`. Clicking Save Copenhagen or Save Ljubljana therefore produces no state change.
findings.impact: Users are offered a meaningful secondary action that silently fails for two of the three destinations.
findings.remediation: Track saved destination IDs and toggle the clicked destination, or remove/disable save controls that are unavailable.

findings.title: Copy link reports success without copying anything
findings.evidence: `handleCopy` only sets `copied` to true and schedules its reset; it never calls the Clipboard API. The modal invokes this handler and changes its label to `Copied` at line 272.
findings.impact: Users can leave the share flow believing the trip link is on their clipboard when it is not.
findings.remediation: Call `navigator.clipboard.writeText` with the displayed URL and show success only after fulfillment, with an error/fallback state.

findings.title: Enabled itinerary actions have no behavior
findings.evidence: The prominent Add activity, Edit day, and View full itinerary buttons have no click handlers; the Dismiss suggestion button is likewise inert.
findings.impact: Several secondary planning actions promise editing, expansion, or dismissal but do nothing, making the finished-product surface misleading.
findings.remediation: Implement the promised state transitions or render these affordances as clearly unavailable rather than enabled buttons.

findings.title: Vote totals contradict each other after voting
findings.evidence: The results introduction hard-codes `18 votes cast`, while the footer renders the derived `totalVotes`. After any vote, the footer becomes `19 of 20 votes in` but the results introduction—and the `Results 18` tab at line 218—remain at 18.
findings.impact: A core voting interaction creates simultaneous, visibly conflicting totals, undermining confidence in the result state.
findings.remediation: Render every vote-total label from the same derived total and update any associated last-vote metadata with the vote transition.

findings.title: Results-view vote buttons have no accessible names
findings.evidence: Each result row ends with a button containing only a Plus or Check icon, without text, `aria-label`, or destination-specific state semantics. This affects all three voting controls in the results view.
findings.impact: Screen-reader users cannot determine which destination an icon-only control votes for or whether activating it will add or remove their vote, creating a repeated barrier in a core task.
findings.remediation: Give each button a destination- and action-specific accessible name and expose its selected state with `aria-pressed` or equivalent semantics.

findings.title: Essential comparison and voting text has insufficient contrast
findings.evidence: The 9px budget/travel labels use `#a0a9a4` on white (approximately 2.41:1), and the 10px Vote text uses white on `#e47d54` (approximately 2.85:1). Both are below the 4.5:1 contrast requirement for normal-sized text and recur on every destination card.
findings.impact: Low-vision users may struggle to read the context for core budget/travel values and the primary voting action.
findings.remediation: Use darker foreground or background colors that provide at least 4.5:1 contrast for these small text sizes.

findings.title: Invite dialog lacks keyboard focus management
findings.evidence: Opening the modal only changes `showInvite`; the dialog has no initial-focus logic, focus trap, Escape handler, background inerting, or focus restoration. From the desktop Share trip opener, subsequent Tab presses can continue through controls behind the appended dialog.
findings.impact: Keyboard users can lose the dialog context, traverse background content while a modal is open, and lose their prior position when it closes.
findings.remediation: Move focus into the dialog on open, contain it while open, close on Escape, make the background inert, and restore focus to the opener.

categories.label: Brief fidelity and functional correctness
evidence: source/src/App.tsx:43-124 supplies three realistic destinations with per-person prices, travel times, ratings, descriptions, tags, and three days of itinerary data.
The supplied desktop and mobile checks both passed destination comparison, budget, travel-time, winner/itinerary reachability, backend-free operation, and browser-error checks.

The desktop vote heuristic failure is not treated as a defect: source/src/App.tsx:234 provides a clear state-changing vote handler and the supplied mobile check successfully observed the button change. The failed desktop heuristic therefore has conflicting source/browser evidence rather than a confirmed broken path.
Secondary save, copy, and itinerary controls do not fulfill their enabled affordances, as detailed in the correctness findings.

categories.label: Reliability and state integrity
evidence: source/src/App.tsx:159-173 uses a stable destination ID for the user's single vote and derives destination and aggregate counts without mutating the sample data.
source/src/App.tsx:234 and source/src/App.tsx:243 both support selecting, switching, and removing the current vote.
Vote-count text is duplicated between derived and hard-coded values at source/src/App.tsx:218 and source/src/App.tsx:242-247, producing the documented state contradiction.

categories.label: Accessibility
evidence: The supplied initial-state checks found no enabled unnamed controls and focused 10 unique desktop and 11 unique mobile controls during their keyboard paths.
source/src/App.tsx:181-270 provides header, main, navigation, section, article, aside, heading, and native button landmarks/elements, with labels on the initial icon-only controls.

The initial-state heuristic did not exercise the results-row icon buttons or modal focus lifecycle; source/src/App.tsx:243 and source/src/App.tsx:272 expose the documented barriers, while source/src/index.css:42 declares repeated sub-threshold text contrast.

categories.label: Maintainability and code clarity
evidence: source/src/App.tsx:29-124 centralizes typed destination data, friends, and itinerary content, while source/src/App.tsx:126-156 extracts repeated avatars and destination artwork.

State and derived vote calculations are localized at source/src/App.tsx:158-177. No separate actionable maintainability defect was found beyond the state and behavior defects assigned to their primary categories.

categories.label: Security, privacy, and dependency compliance
evidence: The supplied dependency comparison passed with no added, removed, or changed packages; remote-reference and external-request lists were empty.

The source uses React-rendered text and contains no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, secret-like configuration, data collection, or external service integration. No actionable security or privacy issue was found.

categories.label: Performance and responsive robustness
evidence: The supplied production output was 290,718 bytes, with 68.46 kB gzip JavaScript and 13.96 kB gzip CSS, and no remote asset requests.

Both supplied viewport checks recorded 0px document-level horizontal overflow, and source/src/index.css:53-54 supplies dedicated tablet and mobile layout rules. No material performance or responsive-robustness defect was demonstrated.

categories.label: Verification readiness
evidence: The supplied `npm run check` completed successfully, including strict TypeScript checking and a Vite production build, with zero browser console or page errors on both defined viewports.

source/package.json:6-10 provides separate typecheck, build, and aggregate check scripts, and source/tsconfig.json:2-17 enables strict TypeScript. No actionable verification-readiness defect was found.

strengths: The core compare-vote-plan experience is present without authentication or backend dependencies, and both supplied viewports reached the destination and itinerary content.
Voting is modeled with stable destination IDs and supports selecting, switching, and removing a vote across both compare and results presentations.
The three-day itinerary is interactive and contains realistic times, activities, reservations, and travel details.
Responsive checks found no horizontal overflow or uncaught browser errors at either 1440×900 or 390×844.
Dependency compliance, strict type checking, and the production build all passed, with no remote references or external requests.
risks: Duplicated vote metadata can diverge immediately after a user action and may cause users to distrust the displayed result.
Core comparison and results controls present repeated contrast and accessible-name barriers for low-vision and screen-reader users.
Several enabled secondary controls either do nothing or falsely report completion, so users cannot reliably infer which planning features are functional.
The invite modal can leave keyboard focus in background content and does not provide a predictable Escape or focus-restoration path.
recommendation: The core Weekender path is deployable and responsive, but the vote total should be made single-source-of-truth before release. Next, label the results controls, correct core text contrast, add modal focus management, and either implement or clearly disable the misleading save, copy, and itinerary actions.
